mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, boosting consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which lifts corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline by at least 5% in annualized forward returns over a 5-year walk-forward test, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO rises for three consecutive months, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 60% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-inflation months are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth outpacing consumer price inflation indicates healthy real demand expansion without overheating, which sustains corporate earnings growth and equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The spread between real GDP growth and inflation fails to predict positive equity returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained nonfarm payroll growth reflects expanding business hiring and economic momentum, which reinforces corporate revenue trajectories and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where monthly payroll growth exceeds 100,000, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent industrial production expansion and unemployment decline signal a productive supply-side boom that outpaces demand-driven inflation, supporting equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises and UNRATE falls in the same month
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline by at least 5% in annualized forward returns over a 5-year walk-forward test, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).
